acceptance_targets: [chapter_02_prose_001] Across the cited lines, pass only if no more than two beats still use the current 'not X / but Y' correction shape and each rewritten moment remains legible on first read as a distinct emotional turn. Fail if three or more of those passages still rely on the same corrective syntax to create emphasis.

[chapter_02_prose_002] In the cited passages, pass only if the revised beats no longer lean on 'looked' as the main transition more than twice and each replaced beat gives the reader a more specific action, sensation, or pressure cue than generic visual attention. Fail if the scene still pivots through a string of near-identical look-verbs.

findings.acceptance_test: Across the cited lines, pass only if no more than two beats still use the current 'not X / but Y' correction shape and each rewritten moment remains legible on first read as a distinct emotional turn. Fail if three or more of those passages still rely on the same corrective syntax to create emphasis.
findings.problem: The chapter leans too often on the same negative-correction turn ('not X, but Y' / 'it wasn't X') to land emotional emphasis. Because the pattern recurs at intake, at Mira's moral turn, at the Tavi alliance beat, and at Nidia's release, it stops feeling sharpened and starts feeling mannered.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the correction-pattern sentences in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:15, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:207, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:443, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:475, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:547 so each beat lands through a different sentence shape or image. Keep the same story facts, but let at least some of these moments resolve through concrete bodily detail, action, or consequence instead of another rhetorical reversal.

findings.acceptance_test: In the cited passages, pass only if the revised beats no longer lean on 'looked' as the main transition more than twice and each replaced beat gives the reader a more specific action, sensation, or pressure cue than generic visual attention. Fail if the scene still pivots through a string of near-identical look-verbs.
findings.problem: Observation beats default too often to characters 'looking' at something or someone. In a chapter whose style profile favors touch, sound, and spatial pressure, that repeated visual hinge starts to feel like connective autopilot and flattens otherwise sharp scene turns.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the repeated 'looked' pivots in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:65, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:87, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:117, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:187, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:231, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:313, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:407, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:429, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:459. Replace most of them with more specific physical behavior, tactile noticing, spatial reaction, or pressure-bearing silence so the scene transitions stay varied without losing clarity.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into biotech grime, erotic awkwardness, debt language, bureaucratic absurdity, and the tenderness of people maintaining one another under bad systems. When choosing between an elegant abstraction and an ugly specific detail, choose the specific detail unless omission itself heightens pressure.
aesthetic_risk_policy.edge_case_resolution: Use restraint only when aftermath, gesture, residue, or changed behavior keeps the full pressure visible. Do not convert morally compromising acts into self-exonerating reflection, and do not flatten exploitative sex, poverty, or violence into generic dystopian mood.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene reaches injury, sex, class shame, or institutional cruelty and the language retreats into tasteful fog, rewrite until the bodily fact, immediate sensation, and social consequence are fully legible on the page.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the norm in spoken English here; uncontracted forms are reserved for legal testimony, managerial performance, deliberate emphasis, or characters whose formality is itself a tactic.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate inspection-room procedural chapters with crowded comic clinic scenes, quiet domestic intervals, erotic pressure, warehouse horror, and civic hearing sequences; consecutive chapters should differ in scene density, dialogue load, and emotional temperature so the book keeps changing how it moves.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, urban, bodily, and unsentimental; technical vocabulary is welcome when embedded in action and cost, not as decorative futurism.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: tight third-person close anchored to Mira Alves; the narration can infer social systems through what she notices, but it should not become omniscient exposition.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate crisp claim-note precision with longer, salt-wet, bodily sentences when feeling, memory, or city pressure breaches Mira's control.
